skill.name: hyperframes
skill.description: Create HyperFrames HTML video compositions with timed clips, GSAP animation, captions, voiceovers, audio-reactive visuals, and scene transitions.
skill.summary: Build HTML-based video compositions with animation, captions, voiceovers, and transitions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: AI review found no prompt injection attempt and no confirmed malicious behavior. Most static findings are false positives caused by CSS hex colors, Markdown examples, GSAP snippets, and design vocabulary; the real residual risks are expected local scripts, filesystem report output, and optional API transcription examples using user-provided keys. The skill is publishable with a low-risk label.

low_findings.title: Documented CLI Workflows and Local Scripts
low_findings.description: The command-related static findings are expected authoring workflows for linting, validation, inspection, transcription, text-to-speech, and animation reports. The executable scripts run local HyperFrames capture and analysis tasks; I did not find evidence of arbitrary shell execution or malicious command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented as local video production and validation steps. The reviewed scripts call HyperFrames producer APIs and do not build shell commands from user input.

low_findings.title: Optional External API Examples Use User-Provided Keys
low_findings.description: The environment variable and network findings are documentation examples for OpenAI and Groq transcription APIs. They use user-provided API key placeholders in curl commands and do not include hardcoded secrets or hidden exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key references are shell placeholders in user-facing instructions. The examples send audio to named transcription APIs only when the user chooses that workflow.

low_findings.title: Local Filesystem Output and Cache Paths
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings relate to expected report directories, generated JSON or PNG outputs, project-relative composition paths, and documented model cache locations. I did not find evidence of hidden credential file access or path traversal used for unauthorized reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed paths are output locations or documented cache directories. Users can choose output directories, so this is a normal local tool capability rather than a confirmed security defect.

dangerous_patterns.title: CSS Hex Color Codes Misclassified as Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Many static external-command and weak-crypto findings are CSS color values in palette and styling reference files. These values are not executable Ruby or shell syntax in context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged tokens appear in palette blocks and CSS examples. They are standard hex color literals, not code execution primitives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown and GSAP Examples Misclassified as Code Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The numerous backtick and command findings occur inside Markdown code fences and JavaScript animation examples. They demonstrate GSAP timeline construction for video compositions and do not execute during skill loading.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content is instructional Markdown and composition source examples. No evidence shows these snippets are executed by the skill itself without user action.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Paths and Cache Paths Misclassified as Sensitive File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The path traversal and hidden-file findings are project-relative path guidance and a documented local model cache path. They do not instruct unauthorized access to sensitive files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The paths are documentation for composition layout and tool caches. I did not find evidence of reads from credential stores, system databases, or hidden user files.

content.value_statement: Video animation work often requires separate tools for timing, captions, audio, and rendering. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build HyperFrames HTML compositions with clear production guidance.
actual_capabilities: Author timed HyperFrames compositions with HTML data attributes and GSAP timelines.
Create animated title cards, overlays, captions, subtitles, and text highlight effects.
Guide text-to-speech narration with Kokoro voices and transcription-based caption workflows.
Add audio-reactive animation using pre-extracted amplitude and frequency data.
Apply scene transitions such as crossfades, wipes, reveals, blur, scale, and shader transitions.
Run local quality checks for layout, contrast, timing, and animation choreography.
limitations: Rendering requires the HyperFrames CLI and its runtime dependencies.
The skill provides authoring guidance; it does not replace video hosting or editing software.
External transcription APIs require user-provided credentials and user approval.
Shader transitions depend on the separate HyperFrames shader transition package.

use_cases.title: Marketing Launch Videos
use_cases.description: Create branded title cards, product scenes, animated callouts, and polished transitions for short promotional videos.

use_cases.title: Training and Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Build captioned educational videos with voiceover, readable typography, and synchronized visual emphasis.

use_cases.title: Event Motion Graphics
use_cases.description: Produce animated speaker intros, conference segments, recap scenes, and audio-reactive presentation visuals.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Title Card
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HyperFrames title card for [TITLE]. Use a defined visual identity, static layout first, and one entrance animation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one animated scene.

prompt_templates.title: Captioned Audio Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HyperFrames composition for [AUDIO_FILE] with synchronized captions, readable typography, and a clean background.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for captions from audio or transcript data.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Scene Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a [NUMBER]-scene HyperFrames video about [TOPIC]. Add entrance animations, transitions between scenes, and validation notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a structured video sequence.

prompt_templates.title: Full Production Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete HyperFrames video plan and composition for [BRIEF]. Include visual identity, captions, voiceover, audio-reactive motion, transitions, and checks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced production request with multiple HyperFrames features.

output_examples.input: Create an animated title card for a product launch.
output_examples.output: A timed HyperFrames scene with brand colors, strong typography, and a GSAP entrance animation.

output_examples.input: Add captions to my narration audio.
output_examples.output: A caption workflow with transcript quality checks, grouped word timing, and readable synchronized caption scenes.
A multi-scene composition plan with transition choices, entrance choreography, and validation steps.

best_practices: Define the visual identity before writing composition HTML.
Build the static layout before adding GSAP animation.
Run HyperFrames validation and inspection before final rendering.
anti_patterns: Do not use generic default colors without a visual identity.
Do not animate from guessed positions before confirming the final layout.
Do not skip transcript review before building synchronized captions.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates HyperFrames HTML compositions for video scenes, captions, animation, voiceover, transitions, and audio-reactive visuals.

faq.question: Does it render the final video by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides composition authoring. Rendering requires the HyperFrames CLI and related runtime tools.

faq.question: Can it add subtitles or captions?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports transcript-based captions, word-level timing, karaoke effects, and readable caption layouts.

faq.question: Can it generate voiceover?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents local Kokoro text-to-speech workflows and how to add the generated audio to a composition.

faq.question: Does it require external APIs?
faq.answer: No. External transcription APIs are optional and require user-provided credentials when selected.

faq.question: What should users check before rendering?
faq.answer: Users should validate layout, contrast, timing, transitions, and transcript quality before final rendering.
